sections.paragraph:
ζηλόω [ζῆλος]

—1. ‘have passionate interest’ in someth., be zealous (for) 1 Cor 12:31; 14:39. —The two uses of ζ. show zeal (for) Gal 4:17a (a positive sense for ζ. is followed by a negative) and 18b (the opponents work for a switch of allegiance from Paul to themselves) constitute a sophisticated combination of the ideas expressed in the two senses of ζῆλος.

—2. to envy, be jealous Ac 7:9; 17:5; 1 Cor 13:4; Js 4:2.
